Hey, welcome back to the next lesson. It's time to dive into some really core content of the straight line system, which are the three tens. Now, just to be clear here, what I'm talking about here with the three tens is that these are those three core elements that must line up in your prospect's mind before you have any chance of closing them. If one of them is missing, you have no chance of closing them at all. Those are the three core elements. Now remember, as we spoke about in the last lesson, it's all about certainty. It's about making your prospect absolutely certain. And when we think of certainty as it relates to sales, we think about what? We think about certainty for the product. They must absolutely, positively love your product. So imagine this. Let's say you are about to ask for the order for the first time. Where would you like your prospect on the certainty scale for that? You want them at a 10 when they love the product? So they think it's going to really improve their lives. It's a great deal. Or would you like them to be at a one? Of course, you want them at a 10. Or as close to a 10 as possible. So that is your first core element of the straight line. It's called your first 10. They must absolutely, positively love your product. And that could be a tangible product, car, boat, house, right? So like that, it could be an intangible product, an idea, a concept, even a vision for the future, whichever the case, they must absolutely, positively love your product on both a logical and an emotional level. Now let me just give you an idea of what that sounds like when you hear it coming from a prospect. So if you were to say to your prospect, so Bill, tell me, what do you think of the program? Let's say you're selling insurance. And Bill says, oh my God, it's the greatest program ever, Jordan. This thing kicks butt. I mean, it's going to cover my family. It barely costs me anything each month. It's got a huge net equity value. I mean, wow, I'm going to feel so good once I get this thing in place. It's going to make my life so much better. Thank you so much. This is an awesome product. I mean, you love that, right? That's what a 10 on the certainty scale sounds like, right? Versus a one where you say, Bill, what do you think of the insurance product? He goes, that's the biggest piece of **** in the world, Jordan. Who in their mind would ever buy this thing? It doesn't cover my needs. It doesn't protect my family. It's overpriced. It smells. I mean, I just feel like it's toxic oozing out of the thing. Get that thing out of my life, please, as quickly as possible. That's a one, right? That's a one on the certainty scale. So that first 10 is they must absolutely, positively love your product. So essentially, as you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close, everything you're saying, everything you're doing is designed to create this airtight kick-ass stream from the Hilltops logical case to get into a 10 about the product. Make sense? Okay, good. So let's say you successfully do that. In other words, let's say you have a financially qualified prospect who loves your product, needs your product, wants your product, he's in pain about your product, he has urgency to buy, right? Is that an absolute 10 on the certainty scale you ask for the order? Here's my question. Will he buy from you? Yes or no? Now answer below, but be careful, but do answer quickly. Okay, you answered. Yes, and you would think that is true, but the reality is it's actually not. See, the correct answer is maybe, as in maybe they'll buy and maybe they won't buy. Okay, you answered no. And while intuitively that probably seems a bit off to me at least, the truth is it was a trick question. See the correct answer is actually maybe, as in maybe they will and maybe they won't. You see, here's the deal. Let's say in the process of you creating this incredible stream from the hilltops, absolute certainty in your prospect's heart and mind. Let's say as you do that, you say something, you do something, something about the way you went about doing it, pisses the prospect off and makes them not trust you or not like you. You totally broke rapport with them. It could be the way you shook hands, the lack of eye contact. It could be the fact that you came up with a grand inquisitor versus the trusted friend and advisor. Something that you did though, really pissed this person off and in terms of the continuum of certainty when it comes to trusting you, they don't like that on one or two. Will they buy from you now? No way. Listen, here's the deal. Take this for logically. We don't buy from people that we don't trust. We just don't. In fact, if you love the product that much and you distrust the salesman, what would you do? You just simply say, thanks buddy. Not interested in you running and go buy from a salesman. You trust the exact same product. That's what you would do. Okay? Here's the point. It's not enough as you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close. It's not enough to just make them absolutely certain about the product logically and emotionally. They also have to be absolutely certain about you in terms of trusting and connecting with you, the salesperson. And that is your second key element, your second 10. They must trust and connect with you. An example of someone that trusted you, imagine if they were talking to their friend about a salesman, oh my God, he is the greatest guy. I mean, he totally has my best interest at heart. I mean, this guy, I would leave him in my house with my kids alone. I know he always does the right thing. He gives great customer service. He's not just there to make a commission. He's just the greatest guy. What an asset he is to my family. That's what it sounds like when someone's at a very high level on the second 10 versus someone that's at a very low level. You'd say, so what do you think of that salesman? Sure, he's a freaking snake in the grass. That is stone cold novice. I know all he cares about is a commission. I'd like to rip his eye money. You get the difference, right? So again, obviously, this is really obvious stuff now, but the way it plays out in the real world of sales is staggeringly important is that it's not enough. They just love your product. They must also trust and connect with you, the salesperson, at a really high level. So let's say you get that done. In other words, let's say you make a kick ass scream from the hilltops, airtight logical presentation. So when you're done there, just totally, totally certain about the product and you did everything right in terms of rapport building, eye contact, all the questions you wish, everything was done in just the right way. So they're saying, you know what, this, wow, I'm at a 10. They won't say it, but they're thinking like, I'm at a 10 for the salesperson. I trust this person. I want them in my life. I can see them being a benefit to me and my family over the long term. I want this guy or girl in my life. So let's say you get now to a 10 about both of those things. So now they're at a 10 about the product and they're at a 10 about you. Here's my next question. Will they buy from you in this case? Let's say you corral those two things up and you ask for the order. Will the prospect buy from you now? Yes, no. And now notice here is also a maybe. Which one would it be? Wow, you answered yes. I think you would have caught on by now. The correct answer is maybe as in maybe they will and maybe they won't. Okay, you answered no, but I thought you would have caught on by now. The correct answer is maybe as in maybe they will and maybe they won't. Bingo, you got it. The correct answer now is maybe as in maybe they will and maybe they won't. Because here's the deal. There's also this third core element, meaning what if they don't trust the company that stands behind the product? See, it's not enough as they trust you, the salesperson. And it's not enough that they love the product. If they don't trust the company that's going to deliver the product or service, that's going to be there for customer service. If they don't have integrity, if they rip people off. I mean, wow, good luck trying to close that person. So that is your third 10. They must trust and connect with the company that stands behind the product. Bottom line. You see, at the end of the day, the way this really plays out in the real world of sales is that as you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close, what you're looking to do is corral a 10, 10, 10 in a single moment in time. And then ask for the order in just the right way. And if you do that, then you have an excellent shot of closing the deal. And again, conversely, if even one of those three 10s is missing, then you basically have no shot of closing the deal at all. Now, that being said, let's think back to the last lesson for a moment in terms of where your prospect lands on the certainty scale when they first enter the sales account. Here's a question. Where is your prospect on the certainty scale as it relates to your product? And for that matter, both you and the company as well. Where are they when they enter? Are they at a five? Are they at a seven, a three, a 10? Which one? Well, I'm not going to play the whole interactivity game with you, because I want to go quickly here. But here's the answer. The answer is who really knows? I mean, how could you know? Unless you're a Crestkin, the mind reader, then you don't know where your prospect lands on the certainty scale when they enter that encounter at that first moment. But here's what you do know. They're somewhere. They're somewhere on the scale between a one and a 10 because they don't come from Mars or Pluto. They come from planet Earth. In other words, they know about cars, about insurance policies, about boats, hats, coaching problems, about the internet, social media, and others. Whatever you happen to be selling, the moment your prospect enters the encounter and realizes what's going on, what product you're selling, their brain will instantly size up all their past experiences, their predispositions, their prejudices, everything their mommy and their daddy told them, they were this big and all of them from their unconscious mind, and it lands them at what they deem to be the appropriate spot on the certainty scale for each of the three tens. Just where? Well, you don't really know, but the point is they land somewhere. And whenever that somewhere on the certainty scale is, you know exactly what your goal is, namely that everything you say and everything you do is designed to get those closest possible to a 10, 10, 10, and then ask for the order in just the right way, which is calmly and elegantly so you can then close them. Now, when they dig into this content in much more detail, we get to the looping module a bit later on, but for now, I want to stop and take a quiz because this content is just really mission critical. So I want you to really take some time to go through the quiz, the exercises there as well, and when you're done, we'll meet in the next lesson, we'll go through the remaining two elements of the straight line system, which will give you all five numbers that aforementioned combination lock that will allow you to close anyone who is closeable. I'll see you soon.
